name: litellm
explanation: LiteLLM Proxy exposes Prometheus metrics on a single canonical path `/metrics` (per upstream docs at https://docs.litellm.ai/docs/proxy/prometheus) at the proxy's HTTP port — `4000` by default in the spec example and the project's documented default. The check inherits `OpenMetricsBaseCheckV2`. The only override is `openmetrics_endpoint.required: false`, so the spec marks it optional, but the example reflects the universal upstream default. The optional `litellm_health_endpoint` augments OpenMetrics with a JSON `/health` poll at the same host:port. This is a textbook pattern A ("OpenMetrics on a known port") with one extra optional auxiliary URL on the same origin — port-scan for Prometheus on 4000/metrics is sufficient.
notes: No auto_conf.yaml ships, but the spec example pins `http://localhost:4000/metrics`, the LiteLLM proxy default. The auxiliary `/health` endpoint sits on the same origin and can be derived if `/metrics` is found.
